oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Foraging - Remember There’s a Learning Curve
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Sullivan, Robin
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This item originally appeared in AFA Watchbird Volume 50 Number 3 (July 2023).The following is an excerpt from the full PDF link.Foraging—Remember There’s a Learning CurveForaging for food items is a very natural, instinctual behavior for psittacines. In a natural setting, parrots may travel great distances to make a search for food. Different species forage in different ways. Some parrots are arboreal foragers while others are ground foragers and some even do both! Foraging is a great form of exercise for birds. In addition, foraging can potentially help to alleviate aberrant behavior like over preening or pacing by offering alternative behaviors.provided in a certain place in their environment and the drive to look elsewhere isn’t present.Click the PDF link associated with this item to view the complete item as originally published.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 50 No. 3 (2023): Watchbird July 2023; 8-9
